​SE C TION 1​
​About This Guide​
​WaiverPro is a web-based platform for managing healthcare regulatory waiver requests​
​from submission through approval. This manual explains how to navigate the​
​application, submit and track waiver requests, manage facilities and users, and obtain​
​support.​
​Who should use this guide​
​This guide is written for​​provider-portal users​​—​​facility administrators and staff who​
​prepare, submit, and monitor waiver applications on behalf of their organisation.​
​Scope​
​This document describes the provider-facing web application. Every screen, label, and​
​button described here reflects the live product. Figures show live account data; the specific​
​records on your screen will differ from the examples pictured.​
​CONVENTIONS​
​Button labels and on-screen text are shown in​​bold​.​​Web addresses and field values are shown​
​in​​monospace​​. Each section also lists the​​URL path​​of the screen it describes, relative to the​
​site’s base address.​
​SE C TION 2​
​Accessing WaiverPro — The Landing Page​
​URL​​/​
​Open WaiverPro in your web browser. The public landing page introduces the platform and​
​lists the waiver programs currently available.​

​Figure 2.1 — The WaiverPro landing page: heading, search bar, and the​​Sign In​​button.​
​Key elements of the landing page:​
​Heading​​— “Healthcare Waiver Management”, with the​​tagline “Streamline your​
​regulatory waiver process from submission to approval”.​
​Search waivers​​— the search box (placeholder​​Search​​waivers​​) filters the program list​
​by name.​
​All Waiver Programs​​— quick-filter buttons name each​​program, and each published​
​program appears as a card showing its name, description,​​Processing Time​,​​Waiver​
​Period​, and an eligibility checklist. A program that​​is not currently open shows a status​
​note (for example, “Expired”, with the dates it was available).​
​Sign In​​— the button in the top-right corner takes​​you to the sign-in screen.​​Footer​​—​
​links to​​Privacy Policy​​and​​Terms of Service​, with​​the line “Copyright © 2025​
​WaiverPro”.​

​Figure 2.2 — The​​All Waiver Programs​​section, listing​​each program as a detailed​
​card.​​To begin, click​​Sign In​​in the top-right corner​​of the page.​
​SE C TION 3​
​Signing In​
​URL​​/login​
​The sign-in screen authenticates you into the provider portal.​

​Figure 3.1 — The sign-in screen.​
​1. Enter your​​Email​​(placeholder​​m@example.com​​).​
​2. Enter your​​Password​. Passwords must be at least​​6 characters​.​
​3. Click​​Login​.​
​If you have forgotten your password, use the​​Forgot​​your password?​​link beside the​
​password field to begin recovery.​
​After a successful sign-in you are taken to the​​My​​Applications​​dashboard.​
​SE C TION 4​
​The Application Workspace & Navigation​
​URL​​Shared by all signed-in pages (​​/dashboard/…​​)​
​Once signed in, every page shares a common layout: a fixed sidebar on the left and a​
​header along the top.​

​Figure 4.1 — The signed-in workspace. The sidebar (left) provides navigation; the header (top) provides search,​
​notifications, and your account menu.​
​The sidebar​
​At the top of the sidebar the brand​​WaiverPro​​appears​​with the label “Healthcare Waiver​
​Management”, followed by the primary action button​​+ New Waiver Request​. The​
​navigation items, in order, are:​
​My Applications​​— your waiver applications dashboard.​
​Facilities​​— facilities you manage or belong to.​
​Action Items​​— pending items that require your attention.​
​User Management​​— manage the users in your organisation.​
​Announcements​​— system and program updates.​
​Settings​​— account and preference settings.​

​Below a​​Support​​heading, four further items appear:​​FAQs​,​​Tickets​,​​Contact​, and​​Take a​
​Tour​​(which launches a guided walkthrough of the interface).​
​The header​
​Search​​— a search box for the current page.​
​Notifications​​— a bell icon with a badge showing the​​number of outstanding action​
​items.​​Account​​— your initials appear as an avatar;​​opening it reveals your email​
​address and a​​Logout​​button.​
​TIP​
​The​​+ New Waiver Request​​button is available from​​the sidebar on every page, so you can​
​start a waiver request at any time.​
​SE C TION 5​
​My Applications​
​URL​​/dashboard/my-applications​
​The​​My Applications​​dashboard is your home screen.​​It lists your waiver​
​applications and summarises them in two charts.​

​Figure 5.1 — The My Applications dashboard: waiver-type tabs, status filters, the applications table, and the two​
​summary charts.​
​Filtering the list​
​Two layers of filters narrow the table, each showing a live count:​
​Waiver-type tabs​​—​​All​,​​Patient Needs Waiver (PNW)​​Request​,​​Program Flexibility​
​(PF) Request​, and​​Workforce Shortage Waiver (WSW)​​Request​.​
​Status chips​​—​​All Applications​,​​Draft​,​​Submitted​,​​Reopen​,​​In Review​,​​Rejected​,​
​Approved​,​​Evaluator Review​,​​Manager Review​,​​Revoked​,​​and​​Expired​.​
​The applications table​
​Applications are listed with the columns​​Waiver ID​,​​Name​,​​Facility​,​​Type​,​​Status​,​
​and​​Created on​. Use the​​Rows per page​​control and​​the page arrows to page through​
​results.​

​Summary charts​
​Applications by Type​​— a doughnut chart breaking your​​applications down by​
​waiver type (PNW, PF, WSW).​
​Applications Overview​​— a doughnut chart breaking​​your applications down by​
​status.​
​SE C TION 6​
​Submitting a New Waiver Application​
​URL​​Opens as a panel over the current page (no dedicated​​URL)​
​Click​​+ New Waiver Request​​in the sidebar to open​​the application panel. A progress bar​
​and the step tabs​​Waiver Type​,​​Select Location​, and​​Fill Application​​show where you are.​
​Figure 6.1 — The​​New Waiver Application​​panel, open​​at step one, “Select Waiver Type”.​
​Step 1 — Waiver Type​
​Under “Select Waiver Type”, choose the waiver request that best matches your needs. Each​
​type —​​Patient Needs Waiver (PNW) Request​,​​Program​​Flexibility (PF) Request​, and​
​Workforce Shortage Waiver (WSW) Request​​— appears​​as a selectable card with a​
​description. A type that is not currently open is marked (for example, “Expired”) and​
​cannot be selected; the panel notes the dates it was available.​

​Step 2 — Select Location​
​Choose the facility (and facility type) the request applies to.​
​Step 3 — Fill Application​
​Complete the request details — contact number, dates, description, supporting documents,​
​and the required acknowledgement — then submit.​
​Use​​Next​​to advance through the steps; you can return​​to an earlier step to make​
​changes, and submit your completed request on the final step. The​​×​​button in the​
​top-right corner closes the panel at any time.​
​SE C TION 7​
​Facilities​
​URL​​/dashboard/facilities​
​The​​Facilities​​page lists the facilities in your organisation​​and tracks the status of their​
​waiver applications.​

​Figure 7.1 — The Facilities page, with status filters, the facilities table, and the Facilities Status Overview.​
​Filters​
​A row of status chips filters by application due-date status:​​All Facilities​,​​Due Soon​,​
​Upcoming​,​​Scheduled​,​​Overdue​, and​​No Status​. You can​​further filter by​​Role​​and​​Type​, or​
​search by name.​
​NOTE​
​The status chips and the​​Status​​/​​License Exp Date​​columns are part of the​​Facilities​
​Status Tracking​​feature, which can be turned on or​​off under​​Settings​​→​​Features​.​
​The facilities table​
​Facilities are listed with the columns​​Name​,​​Type​,​​License Number​,​​License Exp Date​, and​
​Status​. Below the table, the​​Facilities Status Overview​​summarises the portfolio. Use the​
​paging controls to move through results.​
​SE C TION 8​

​Action Items​
​URL​​/dashboard/action-items​
​The​​Action Items​​page collects pending items that​​require your attention.​
​Figure 8.1 — The Action Items page, with the items table and the status summary chart.​
​Items are listed with the columns​​ID​,​​Name​,​​Status​,​​Priority​, and​​Due Date​. Each​​Name​
​links to the relevant record so you can act on it. You can filter the list by​​Status​​and​
​Priority​​using the controls at the top right.​
​Status​​values include​​Done​​and​​In Process​.​
​Priority​​values are​​High​,​​Medium​, and​​Low​.​
​Due Date​​shows how far an item is overdue or when​​it is due.​
​A summary doughnut chart shows the total number of action items broken down by​
​priority. The same outstanding count is reflected by the notification badge in the header.​
​SE C TION 9​

​User Management​
​URL​​/dashboard/user-management​
​The​​User Management​​page lets organisation administrators​​view and manage the​
​people who can access WaiverPro on behalf of the organisation.​
​Figure 9.1 — The User Management page.​
​Two tabs separate current​​Member​s from​​Pending Invites​,​​each showing a count. The table​
​lists each user with the columns​​Name​,​​Email​,​​Associated​​Facilities​,​​Last Updated​, and​
​Actions​​(edit and remove). To add someone, click​​Invite​​User​​in the top-right corner. Use the​
​search box to locate a specific user, and the paging controls to move through the list.​
​SE C TION 1 0​
​Announcements​
​URL​​/dashboard/announcements​
​The​​Announcements​​page keeps you informed: “Stay up​​to date with important​
​system and program updates.”​

​Figure 10.1 — The Announcements page.​
​Each announcement shows its title, a status badge —​​Active​,​​Upcoming​, or​​Expired​​— an​
​optional priority badge (​High​,​​Medium​, or​​Low​), and​​its date. Click an announcement to​
​expand and read its full detail. A search box helps you find a specific announcement.​
​SE C TION 1 1​
​Support — FAQs, Tickets & Contact​
​The​​Support​​section of the sidebar offers three ways​​to get help.​
​FAQs​
​URL​​/dashboard/faqs​
​The​​FAQs​​page answers common questions about WaiverPro.​​Click any question to​
​expand its answer.​

​Figure 11.1 — The FAQs page.​
​Support Tickets​
​URL​​/dashboard/tickets​
​The​​Tickets​​page (“Support Tickets — Track and manage​​your support tickets.”) lists your​
​tickets as cards showing the ticket number, status and priority badges, and the​
​created/updated times. Filter by​​Status​​and​​Priority​,​​and open any ticket with​​View Details​.​
​Click​​+ New Ticket​​to raise one.​

​Figure 11.2 — The Support Tickets page.​
​In the​​Create New Support Ticket​​panel, provide a​​Title​, choose the​​Related​
​Application​, select a​​Category​​and​​Sub Category​, add​​a​​Description​, attach any files,​
​and click​​Create Ticket​.​

​Figure 11.3 — The Create New Support Ticket panel.​
​Contact​
​URL​​/dashboard/contact​
​The​​Contact​​page (“Get in touch with our support team”)​​provides a message form and the​
​support team’s direct details.​

​Figure 11.4 — The Contact page.​
​Support contact details:​
​Email:​​support@waiver-pro.org​
​Phone:​​+1 (555) 123-4567​
​Address:​​123 Healthcare Plaza, Suite 400, New York,​​NY 10001​
​Business hours:​​Monday – Friday, 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM​​EST (closed weekends)​
​Messages sent through the form are typically answered within 24 hours during business​
​days.​
​SE C TION 1 2​
​Settings​
​URL​​/dashboard/settings​
​The​​Settings​​page (“Manage your account and preferences”)​​shows your organisation​
​and is organised into four cards.​

​Figure 12.1 — The Settings page.​
​Organisation​​— the organisation your account belongs​​to is shown at the top of the page.​
​Profile Information​​— your first name, last name,​​email (read-only), and phone number.​
​Click​​Save Changes​​to update.​
​Notifications​​— toggles for​​Email Notifications​,​​Status​​Updates​, and​​Action Items​.​
​Security​​— change your password. A new password must​​be at least 6 characters​
​long. Click​​Change Password​​to apply.​
​Features​​— toggle experimental or upcoming features:​​Facilities Status Tracking​
​(shows application due dates and status on the Facilities page) and the​​FAQ Assistant​
​Chatbot​​(off by default).​
​SE C TION 1 3​

​Legal — Privacy & Terms​
​URL​​/privacy​​·​​/terms​
​The​​Privacy Policy​​and​​Terms of Service​​are linked​​from the footer of the landing page​
​and are available without signing in.​
​Figure 13.1 — The Privacy Policy page.​

​Figure 13.2 — The Terms of Service page.​
​Each page is dated “Last updated: December 2025” and provides a​​Back to Home​​link.​
